body.text: , applied to denote a number, [namely
Nine,
] is masc. ; and ↓
, so applied, is fem.: (S:) the latter is also written ↓
, with fet-h to the
; and is thus pronounced in the Kur xxxviii. 22, (Bd, MF,) accord. to one reading. (Bd.) You say
Nine men
], and
Nine women
]. (K.) When it means the things numbered, not the amount of the number,
is imperf. decl., being regarded as a proper name: thus you say,
Nine things are more than eight things
]. (TA.) It is said in the Kur [xvii. 103],

body.text_bw: wa laqado A=tayonaA muwsaY tisoEa A=yaAtK
body.text_ar: وَ لَقَدْ ا=تَيْنَا مُوسَى تِسْعَ ا=يَاتٍ

body.text: And we formerly gave unto Moses nine evident signs;

generally understood to mean the principal miracles which he was empowered to perform, and which are differently enumerated in the K and other works; but by some supposed to mean statutes]. (K, * TA.) ―         -b2-  In
, which is masc., and
, which is fem., [each signifying
Nineteen,
] each of the two words ends with fet-h in every case, because they are two nouns which are regarded as one noun. (TA.) The former is pronounced by some of the Arabs
: and the latter, thus in the dial. of El-Hijáz
[and of most of the Arabs], is pronounced
in the dial. of Nejd. (S in art.
.) In the Kur lxxiv. 30, some read,
, making the
in
quiescent, instead of
, from a dislike of this consecution of vowels in what is like one word. (Bd, TA. *)

entries.lexicon_en: Al-Rāghib al-Isfahānī, al-Mufradāt fī Gharīb al-Qurʾān (d. c. 1109 CE)
entries.lexicon_ar: المفردات في غريب القرآن للراغب الأصفهاني
entries.body_html:
تسعة
التِّسْعَة في العدد معروفة وكذا التِّسْعُون، قال تعالى: تِسْعَةُ رَهْطٍ [النمل/ 48] ، تِسْعٌ وَتِسْعُونَ نَعْجَةً [ص/ 23] ، ثَلاثَ مِائَةٍ سِنِينَ وَازْدَادُوا تِسْعاً [الكهف/ 25] ، عَلَيْها تِسْعَةَ عَشَرَ [المدثر/ 30] ، والتِّسْع: من أظماء الإبل ، والتُّسْع: جزء من تسعة ، والتُّسَعُ ثلاث ليال من الشهر آخرها التاسعة ، وتَسَعْتُ القوم: أخذت تسع أموالهم، أو كنت لهم تاسعا.
